feat: Analyze as Group button for MRS in Variable Browser

When a variable group is selected in the Explore tab, a new
"Analyze group (N vars)" button appears below the dropdown.
Clicking it runs a multiple_response analysis on all variables
in the group, producing a consolidated frequency table.

Connects existing MRS infrastructure (backend multiple_response
executor + frontend ExploreRunRequest.mrs_variables) to the
Variable Groups UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add visual grid panels for consistent column layout

Each column in the 3 content rows is now wrapped in a .cell panel
with subtle background (#151920), border (#252d3d), and border-radius.
CSS Grid stretch makes all cells in a row equal height, creating a
clean, professional grid layout. Tightened spacing to keep it under
800px viewport height (799px).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: use per-invocation subdirectory in Compiler (#309)

Create a unique subdirectory (GUID-based) under the shared Typewriter
temp dir for each Compiler instance. Compiled DLLs and copied reference
assemblies are written to this subdirectory instead of the shared root,
isolating each invocation's files and enabling simple cleanup via
Directory.Delete(subdir, recursive: true).

Closes #302

commit.message:
Add triage step: answer questions, ask for clarification, or execute

Before launching the full executor pipeline, a quick LLM triage call
classifies user input as:
- answer: question about clive itself → respond directly, no tmux
- clarify: task is ambiguous → ask a question, wait for user reply
- execute: task is clear → proceed to planner/executor

This prevents wasting tokens on vague tasks and lets clive answer
questions about itself without spinning up tmux sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: clive
repository.description: Give an LLM a terminal and a keyboard. Let it read the screen, decide what to type, type it, watch what happens, repeat. That's the entire loop. No schemas. No tool definitions. No MCP server to implement. Just text in, text out — the same interface developers have used for 50 years, now operated by a language model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[CHA-2578] chore: relax faraday and sorbet-runtime version constraints (#200)

* chore: relax faraday and sorbet-runtime version constraints

- faraday: ~> 2.12.2 -> ~> 2.12 (unblocks security fix in 2.14.1)
- faraday-multipart: ~> 1.1.0 -> ~> 1.1 (allows 1.2.x, Ruby 4 compat)
- faraday-net_http_persistent: ~> 2.3.0 -> ~> 2.3
- sorbet-runtime: ~> 0.5.11820 -> >= 0.5.11820, < 2 (allows 0.5.x and 0.6.x)
- sorbet (dev): ~> 0.5 -> ~> 0.6

Closes #196, closes #189

* chore: add Ruby 4.0 to CI matrix, run lint checks on 3.4 and 4.0

* fix: update sorbet RBI stubs for Sorbet 0.6 compatibility

- Remove conflicting method stubs from hidden.rbi (now in Sorbet 0.6 stdlib)
- Add typed: true headers to gem RBIs missing sigils
- Ignore sorbet/rbi/sorbet-typed in sorbet config (stale 0.5 community stubs)
- Delete sorbet/rbi/sorbet-typed (ignored, 1071 lines of stale stubs removed)

* chore: make lint configurable via matrix flag instead of hardcoded ruby version check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* enabled lint check on ruby 3.4 as well

* fix: tighten sorbet-runtime upper bound from < 2 to < 1

Current sorbet versions are 0.5.x and 0.6.x, both of which are < 1.
Using < 2 was unnecessarily wide and would allow a hypothetical 1.x
series without explicit opt-in.

fix(cache): update lastSyncTimeCache on empty API responses

When API returned 0 results (most tokens), the cache stayed empty,

commit.message:
fix(blog): lower generation temperature + AI self-review fact-check pass

- generate_outline(): temperature 0.4 (was default 1.0)
- generate_article(): temperature 0.3 (was default 1.0) — reduces hallucination
- anthropic_adapter: new fact_check_content() method uses claude-haiku at
  temperature=0.0 to deterministically list claims the model isn't confident
  about; only first 6000 chars processed to keep cost low
- admin_blog.py: fact_check_content() + generate_image_prompt() now run in
  parallel via asyncio.gather; AI flags merged with regex flags (deduped)
  so flagged_stats combines both detection layers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): corriger app root vers bookmi_app/bookmi/ (chemin réel du web server)

diagnostic révèle que public_html/index.php charge depuis:
  $appPath = '/home/u726808002/bookmi_app/bookmi'

Tous les builds précédents ciblaient bookmi_app/ (root du repo) au lieu
de bookmi_app/bookmi/ (sous-dossier Laravel). Le route cache était donc
généré au mauvais endroit et jamais lu par le web server → 404 sur toutes
les nouvelles routes.

Correctifs:
- composer/migrate/optimize tournent désormais dans bookmi_app/bookmi/
- manifest Vite copié vers bookmi_app/bookmi/public/build/
- suppression des étapes rsync inutiles vers domains/bookmi.click/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(inbound): auto-pausa quando operador envia mensagem pelo WhatsApp

Quando o operador envia uma mensagem manualmente pelo WhatsApp Business
(Evolution detecta key.fromMe = true), o workflow agora:
  1. Detecta isOperatorTakeover: true (já existia no Parse)
  2. Novo nó "É Operador?" (Filter) bifurca o fluxo
  3. Supabase - Pausa Conversa seta paused=true no remote_jid
  4. SDR silenciada automaticamente — atendente não precisa fazer nada

Fluxo anterior: fromMe = true → ignore silencioso (sem pausar)
Fluxo novo:     fromMe = true → paused=true → SDR bloqueada

commit.message:
Initial working WASI binary — pdf_oxide wrapped for wasm32-wasip1

Reads a PDF from stdin, writes markdown (or text) to stdout. Supports
--format, --pages, and --tables-only flags. Compiles to 3.5MB with LTO.
Tested with wasmtime against a real PDF.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: pdf-oxide-wasi
repository.description: pdf_oxide compiled to WASI — extract text, tables, and markdown from PDFs via stdin/stdout. Works with wasmtime, wasmer, and any WASI runtime.

- Short (10Q) / Long (15Q) format selector on intro screen

Co-authored-by: Leo <leo@openclaw.ai>

* feat: add skill-security-auditor POWERFUL-tier skill (#230)

Security audit and vulnerability scanner for AI agent skills before installation.

Scans for:
- Code execution risks (eval, exec, os.system, subprocess shell injection)
- Data exfiltration (outbound HTTP, credential harvesting, env var extraction)
- Prompt injection in SKILL.md (system override, role hijack, safety bypass)
- Dependency supply chain (typosquatting, unpinned versions, runtime installs)
- File system abuse (boundary violations, binaries, symlinks, hidden files)
- Privilege escalation (sudo, SUID, cron manipulation, shell config writes)
- Obfuscation (base64, hex encoding, chr chains, codecs)

Produces clear PASS/WARN/FAIL verdict with per-finding remediation guidance.
Supports local dirs, git repo URLs, JSON output, strict mode, and CI/CD integration.

Includes:
- scripts/skill_security_auditor.py (1049 lines, zero dependencies)
- references/threat-model.md (complete attack vector documentation)
- SKILL.md with usage guide and report format

Tested against: rag-architect (PASS), agent-designer (PASS), senior-secops (FAIL - correctly flagged eval/exec patterns).

Co-authored-by: Leo <leo@openclaw.ai>

- Add standalone plugin entry for skill-security-auditor in marketplace.json
- Update engineering-advanced-skills plugin description to include it
- Update skill counts: 85→86 across README, CHANGELOG, marketplace

commit.message:
feat(feishu): send help message when users join group (Issue #676) (#690)

## Summary
- Add `handleUserJoinedGroup` method to WelcomeService
- Update `handleChatMemberAdded` in FeishuChannel to distinguish between:
  - Bot being added to group -> send welcome message (existing)
  - Users joining existing group -> send help message (new)
- Add `isGroupChatId` helper method to check chat type by ID prefix
- Add 5 new tests for `handleUserJoinedGroup`

## Problem
When new users join a group chat that already has the bot, they don't
receive any guidance on how to use the bot's features.

## Solution
1. Detect when non-bot users join a group (not when bot itself is added)
2. Send help/welcome message to introduce bot capabilities to new users
3. Support optional custom help message via `generateHelpMessage` config

## Changes
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| src/platforms/feishu/welcome-service.ts | Add `handleUserJoinedGroup` method |
| src/channels/feishu-channel.ts | Update `handleChatMemberAdded` logic |
| src/platforms/feishu/welcome-service.test.ts | Add 5 tests |

## Test Results
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| TypeScript | ✅ Pass |
| Feishu Tests | ✅ 171 passed |
| New Tests | ✅ 5 passed |

Fixes #676

Co-authored-by: hs3180 <claude@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Create local session for incoming remote SESSION_OPEN envelopes

When a remote node opens a session to a local handle, DeliverToLocal
now creates the session in the local store so the receiving agent can
resolve or send on it. Previously only the sender's daemon created the
session, causing "session not found" errors on the receiver.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tests: adapt pjdfstest for append-only FUSE filesystem

Remove tests for unsupported operations on the FUSE mount:
- chflags, chmod, chown (no permission/attribute changes)
- ftruncate, truncate (no truncate support)
- link, symlink (no hard/symlinks)
- mkfifo, mknod (no special files)
- posix_fallocate (no fallocate)
- utimensat (no timestamp changes)
- granular permission tests

Simplify remaining tests (mkdir, open, rename, rmdir, unlink) to
match the subset of POSIX semantics the FUSE filesystem supports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(poc): content explorer, prompt improvements, and Bedrock model fix

Infrastructure:
- Fix Bedrock model to versioned cross-region inference profile
  (us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0) — short alias rejected
  by Bedrock Runtime
- Expand IAM policy to cover all 3 US regions for cross-region routing
- Add updated model IDs to metrics cost pricing table

Prompts:
- Add leaf-tag constraint to system prompt (Layer 1) — prevents model
  using category/vertical names as tags (e.g. food-preservation)
- Update video (Gemini) prompt with same leaf-tag constraint, confidence
  scoring guide, and breadth-first scan across all 6 verticals

Content Explorer (new feature):
- GET /api/explorer — browse/filter by tag(s) and content type
- GET /api/explorer/[contentId]/related — Jaccard similarity scoring
- POST /api/review/[contentId]/approve — save human-reviewed tags
- Explorer page with tag filter sidebar, type toggle, results grid,
  and related content panel
- ContentCard, TagFilter, TypeFilter, RelatedPanel components
- lib/relatedness.ts — pure Jaccard similarity utility
- Nav link added to layout

Docs:
- poc-walkthrough-for-line-manager.md — non-technical stakeholder brief
- technical-stack-overview.md — full technical stack reference

Agent:
- Add stakeholder-technical-communicator agent definition

commit.message:
feat: sessionid cookie injection to bypass IP blacklist

- database.py: add sessionid_cookie column to upsert_account(), add update_account_sessionid()
- main.py: accept sessionid_cookie in /accounts/add, add POST /accounts/{id}/sessionid endpoint
- accounts.html: sessionid column in table, sessionid registration modal with extraction guide, sessionid field in add modal
- crawler.py: _make_client() already updated to prioritize sessionid login

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: voice command trust boundary, Mission Controller, Autonomy Controller, and Vosk offline wake

- Add vosk-browser WASM wake word engine (VoiceCommandBridge + VoskWakeEngine) with IPC trust boundary — renderer reports raw phrase, main validates against allowlist and broadcasts sanitized command
- Wire MissionController and AutonomyController init in ipc.ts; forward plan/step/complete/failed events to renderer
- Add CommandDispatcher in renderer for typed and voice mission command interception
- Expose mission:start/approve_plan/approve_step/rollback, council:consensus/consensus_meta, system:health, command:audit, and voice:reportWakePhrase IPC handlers in preload
- Replace WakeWordListener with VoiceCommandBridge in Chat.tsx; route voice mission commands to MissionController
- Add core/ modules: autonomy, commands, config, experiments, logging, mission, orchestrator
- Add vosk-browser dependency

commit.message:
Unify navigation layout, merge account into profile, fix registration gate

- Add AppShell with desktop sidebar (220px) and mobile bottom nav
- Make feed the homepage (/), redirect /feed and /account
- Merge account page into profile with linked accounts, subscription, credits
- Fix registration gate: disable Swiper touch when gate shown, raise BottomNav z-index above gate
- Add download system (schema, API route, service, hook, button)
- Design polish: NSFW toggle red state, glass CreditCounter, creator avatar in feed, 18+ badge on cards, view counts in feed, cursor-pointer on all actions
- Remove category filters from mobile feed (desktop sidebar only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate SQLite write contention from concurrent ping threads

Ping threads now return data tuples instead of writing to DB individually.
Scheduler collects all results and does a single batch INSERT, avoiding
lock contention. Added busy_timeout=5000 as safety net.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restaurants as real Streamlit nav page — identical to Progress/Batch Ops

Registered as st.Page with restaurant icon, sits in same nav element.
Clicking it expands restaurant sub-list in sidebar. Removed all
custom button hacks — it's a native nav link now.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add upward escalation protocol to knowledge hierarchy

Fleet level is not write-only from top. A rare, filtered path exists:
Projet/Métier → Fleet when a discovery is universal, fundamental,
and reproducible across 2+ projects or domains.

Hard constraint: human-checked, no exceptions. Agents flag candidates
in to-architect.md [ESCALADE-FLEET], lordzurp validates before encoding.

High frequency of escalations = classification rule misapplied.

fix: Improve error handling and add equity field fallbacks

- Add robust error message extraction in frontend financeApi.ts
- Handle various error response formats (structured, string, unknown)
- Update server/utils/transformer.ts with equity field variants
- Add getErrorMessage helper to server/utils/errors.ts
- Update server routes to use getErrorMessage

This ensures "[object Object]" is never shown as an error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add AI security modules, Phase 1 automation tools, volume gates, and compliance email alerts

Security (backend/src/security/):
- PII redaction for AI prompts (SSN, EIN, bank accounts, routing numbers)
- LLM input sanitization with prompt injection prevention
- Circuit breaker (rate limits, chain depth, event dedup, budget cap)
- Financial decision validator (quick pay, invoice, amount sanity checks)

AI Infrastructure (backend/src/ai/):
- Centralized Claude client with integrated security pipeline
- Volume-gated deployment system (4 gates based on load count)
- System prompts for email classifier, check-call parser, document OCR, morning briefing

Phase 1 Automation (backend/src/automation/):
- Email classifier (Haiku) with keyword-matching fallback
- Check-call reply parser (Haiku) with pattern-matching fallback
- Document OCR processor (Sonnet vision) with manual-task fallback
- Morning briefing generator (Sonnet) with plain-text fallback

Compliance Enhancements:
- Full carrier vetting endpoint (POST /carriers/:id/full-vet)
- Chameleon fraud detection alert emails
- FMCSA suspension notification emails to carriers and admins
- Load status tracking with updatedBy/updatedAt

Schema: AutomationEvent table + AIApiUsage indexes
API: Gate status, feature unlock check, circuit breaker status endpoints
Docs: AI Deployment Gates, Competitive Benchmarks, Security Playbook, Agent Testing
